GlobalTech Manufacturing, a defense contractor with 12,000+ employees across 15 facilities, operates a comprehensive classified information processing environment supporting multiple government contracts valued at $3.2B annually. The organization maintains primary data processing operations at their Virginia headquarters facility housing TOP SECRET/SCI systems, with their designated alternate processing site located at their secondary facility in Richmond, Virginia, approximately 45 miles away. The Chief Information Security Officer implemented citizenship requirements per MA-5.3 ensuring all personnel performing maintenance on classified systems are verified U.S. citizens with current documentation including birth certificates, passports, and security clearance validation, with emergency maintenance procedures explicitly prohibiting non-citizen access under any circumstances. The organization deployed NIAP-approved protection profiles per SA-4.7 for all commercial security products including firewalls evaluated against Network Device Protection Profile v2.2, VPN solutions meeting IPsec Virtual Private Network (VPN) Client Protection Profile v1.0, and database management systems validated against Database Management System Protection Profile v4.0, with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated cryptographic modules for products without applicable NIAP profiles. The network security team implemented comprehensive extrusion detection capabilities per SC-7.9 with advanced threat detection systems monitoring all outbound traffic at managed interfaces, successfully identifying and blocking denial-of-service attack patterns, spoofed source addresses, and malicious code in outbound communications, with complete audit logging of associated internal users within 5 minutes of blocking actions and investigation procedures completing within 4 hours for high-severity threats. The media protection team established robust equipment testing procedures per MP-8.2 with quarterly testing schedules for all media downgrading equipment and semi-annual testing for downgrading procedures, maintaining comprehensive documentation within 5 business days of test completion and immediately isolating failed equipment until successful remediation verification. The organization implemented automated POAM management systems per CA-5.1 providing real-time accuracy and currency with 99.7% uptime during business hours, synchronizing vulnerability data every 12 hours, and generating alerts for critical vulnerabilities within 2 hours of discovery. Access control policies per AC-20.5 strictly prohibit use of organization-controlled portable storage devices on external systems, with technical controls implemented through endpoint protection and comprehensive device inventory tracking throughout lifecycle management. The supply chain team maintains approved component disposal procedures per SR-12 using NIST SP 800-88 approved sanitization methods, witnessed destruction for regulated data with third-party certification, and approved vendor partnerships maintaining current security certifications. Information management systems per CM-12.1 deploy automated discovery tools across all system components with monthly production scans, quarterly accuracy validation maintaining 96% effectiveness, and alert generation within 2 hours when protected information is discovered in unauthorized locations. The facilities team operates automatic fire suppression systems per PE-13.2 in all data centers and server rooms with independent power sources, but notification to designated personnel occurs within 3 minutes of activation due to legacy communication infrastructure limitations currently under modernization.

scenarios.scenario-detail:
CyberSecure Financial Services, a multinational financial institution with 25,000+ employees processing sensitive financial data across cloud and hybrid environments, maintains comprehensive security controls across their global operations. The organization established primary processing operations in New York with alternate processing sites in London, UK, separated by over 3,000 miles to ensure adequate geographic separation from natural disasters and infrastructure failures per CP-7.1, with current threat assessments updated quarterly and comprehensive documentation justifying separation adequacy based on specific threat scenarios including both physical and logical separation requirements. All personnel performing maintenance on classified systems are verified U.S. citizens per MA-5.3 with current documentation including birth certificates, passports, and security clearance validation, with emergency maintenance procedures explicitly prohibiting non-citizen access under any circumstances and comprehensive background verification processes. The network security architecture implements advanced extrusion detection capabilities per SC-7.9 monitoring all outbound communications at managed interfaces, successfully identifying and blocking threatening traffic including denial-of-service patterns, spoofed addresses, and malicious code, with complete audit logging of internal users within 5 minutes and investigation procedures completing within 4 hours for high-severity and 24 hours for medium-severity threats. Media protection operations maintain quarterly equipment testing schedules per MP-8.2 for all downgrading equipment with comprehensive procedure validation, complete documentation within 5 business days, and immediate equipment isolation upon test failures until successful remediation verification. The organization deployed comprehensive automated POAM management per CA-5.1 with real-time accuracy and currency, 99.8% system uptime during business hours, vulnerability data synchronization every 18 hours, automated cross-validation with vulnerability scanners and asset management systems, and critical vulnerability alerting within 3 hours of discovery with complete audit logging maintained for over 1 year. Access control policies per AC-20.5 strictly prohibit organization-controlled portable storage device usage on external systems with robust technical enforcement, comprehensive device inventory tracking, immediate incident reporting requirements within 24 hours, and formal exception processes requiring CISO approval with compensating controls. Component disposal procedures per SR-12 utilize NIST SP 800-88 approved sanitization methods with documentation within 24 hours, FIPS 140-2 Level 3 cryptographic material destruction, witnessed destruction for regulated data, and development component disposal within 35 days of project completion using approved vendors maintaining current certifications. Information discovery systems per CM-12.1 deploy automated tools across all components processing organizational data with monthly production scans, configuration change scanning within 48 hours, comprehensive information type identification including PII and financial data, unauthorized location alerting within 4 hours, and quarterly accuracy validation maintaining 97% effectiveness with report reviews within 5 business days. Facilities security maintains automatic fire suppression systems per PE-13.2 across all data centers with automatic activation, emergency responder notification within 1 minute, independent power sources for notification systems, and quarterly testing of activation and notification functions ensuring continuous protection for unstaffed facilities operating independently.
